semanticCore: Contentment, begging submissively, keeping head raised, mask, or veil
primaryMeaning: Begging submissively or keeping head raised/stretched
quranicRelevance: Feeding the submissive beggar (Qani' - 'al-qani'a'), and raising heads in terror ('muqni'ee ru'oosihim').

keyDerivations.meaning: Contented beggar, submissive solicitor, or one who accepts charity quietly

keyDerivations.form: Form IV Verb (active part. pl: muqni'ee)
keyDerivations.meaning: To raise the head, stretch neck upward, stare blankly, or look up in panic

figurativeOrRareUsages: مُهْطِعِينَ مُقْنِعِي رُؤُوسِهِمْ لَا يَرْتَدُّ إِلَيْهِمْ طَرْفُهُمْ (14:43): Racing forward, keeping their heads raised/stretched upward (in panic), their gaze not returning to them (terror of Resurrection).
discardedNoise: Personal satisfaction and contentment (Qana'ah) - though related to 'Qani', distinct conceptual usage.
summaryEn: The root represents dual states of submissive raising and content waiting, detailing the quiet poor who ask without demands (Qani') and sinners raising heads in terror (Muqni'ee).
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر مد العنق ورفع الرأس ذعراً، والرضا بالقليل طلباً وخضوعاً (قنع)، واصفاً السائل المحتاج المتعفف (القانع)، ورعب المقبولين رافعي الرؤوس.
rawText: تَقَّنَعَ بِشَىْءٍ 5 He was content with a thing. (K, voce تعصّب.) قَانِعٌ , as used in the Kur, xxii. 37, accord. to some, One who asks, or begs. (TA, art. عر.) قُنْعَانٌ [not قُنْعَانُ, as in the CK,] With whom one is contented, or satisfied, (S, K,) like 🡻مَقْنَعٌ, (S, K,) in respect of his judicial decision, or his evidence: (K:) used a like as mase. and fem. and sing. and pl. (S, K) and dual. (S.) قِنَاعٌ A woman's covering worn over the خِمَار [or head-covering]; (Msb;) a woman's headcovering, wider than the 🡻مَقْنَعَة. (S, K.) === قِنَاعُ القَلْبِ The integument of the heart; the pericardium. (Mgh in art. خلع; and K.) مَقْنَعٌ : see قُنْعَانٌ === إِنَّ فِى ذٰلِكَ لَمَقْنَعًا [Verily in that is a sufficiency]. (S, M, in art. سود.) مُقْنِعٌ : see صَافِحٌ. مِقْنَعَةٌ [and مِقْنَعٌ, PS] A woman's head-veil. (MA, PS.)
